commit.message:
fix: chat windows no longer grow infinitely — bounded layout + flex scroll

Root cause: .layout had min-height:100vh (unbounded) so .content and
.main-area had no height ceiling. flex:1 + overflow-y:auto on message
containers was useless — parent chain had no upper bound.

Fixes:
- main.css: .layout → height:100vh + overflow:hidden (was min-height:100vh)
  .content: add min-height:0 so grid child respects parent constraint
  .main-area already has flex:1 + overflow-y:auto → normal pages still scroll
- ideation.css: .idea-chat → add flex:1 + min-height:0 so it fills bounded
  parent instead of growing. .idea-messages already has overflow-y:auto ✅
- mkt_ideation.css: same fix for .mkt-chat (used by group_ideation too)

All chat pages (Jarvis /cto, agent /chat, conversation, ideation,
group_ideation) now scroll within their message container instead of
stretching the window.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent contradictory BUY/SELL signals

Two issues fixed:
1. SELL signal no longer fires when price is below buy price — recommending
   a sell at a loss makes no sense. A bearish MA crossover below buy price
   is now suppressed (logged only).
2. BUY dip signal is suppressed when MA trend is bearish (MA15 < MA60).
   Buying into a downtrend compounds losses. The user sees a clear warning
   instead: 'Dip detected but trend is bearish — wait for stabilisation.'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: Aggressive cache busting with query strings

- Added timestamp query string to all assets in index.html
- Strips query string from file path lookup to prevent 404s
- Ensures browser never uses cached CSS/JS from older versions
- Solves blank page issue from persistent browser cache
- Every page load gets fresh assets with new timestamp

HTTP headers also strengthened:
- Added Pragma: no-cache and Expires: 0 for index.html
- Maintains 1h cache for static assets (content-addressed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace HTML template with modern Tailwind CSS SaaS landing page + add /privacy route

- New landing page: sticky navbar, hero (gradient), problem/solution/features/
  users/mission/about/responsible-AI sections, and dark footer
- Tailwind CSS via CDN added; existing .tabs/.card/.btn/.output/.spinner/
  .char-counter/.std-desc/.state-selector-bar CSS preserved in <style> block
- All 14 tool panels (lesson, feedback, diff, policy, email, integrity,
  assessment_prep, parent, unit, iep504, quiz, rubric, refine, sitefb)
  preserved with exact element IDs
- All JavaScript preserved exactly: STATE_CONFIG, updateStandards,
  showStdDesc, onStateChange, show, cp, focusMode, call, DOMContentLoaded
- stateSelect dropdown with all 50 states + worldwide option preserved
- {{ state_config_json | safe }} Jinja2 expression preserved
- PRIVACY_HTML variable + @app.route('/privacy') added before __main__
- Privacy page includes full policy text (FERPA, COPPA, data, terms)
  with consistent Tailwind navbar/footer

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: change container port from 80 to 8080

.NET 10 defaults to listening on port 8080. The Kubernetes deployment
was configured for port 80, causing health probes to fail and Kubernetes
to repeatedly restart the pod.

Changes:
- containerPort: 80 -> 8080
- livenessProbe port: 80 -> 8080
- readinessProbe port: 80 -> 8080
- Service targetPort: 80 -> 8080 (external port stays 80)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Harden financial precision, fix transaction semantics, and batch log cleanup (#46)

* Initial plan

* Harden ParametricInsurance oracle: fix transaction race, counter cache, and float precision

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Harden financial precision, fix transaction semantics, and batch cleanup

- parametric_insurance.rb: add require 'bigdecimal' (Ruby 3.4+ stdlib removal safety)
- blockchain_minting_service.rb: fix to_wei float precision loss — BigDecimal(amount.to_s) * 10**18
- naas_contract.rb: use cluster.active_trees_count counter cache; use Rational(1,5) instead of Float 0.20
- insurance_payout_worker.rb: replace return with next inside transaction block (semantic trap fix)
- insight_generator_service.rb: replace delete_all with in_batches(of: 10_000) to avoid table lock
- maintenance_record.rb / maintenance/form.rb: fix trailing empty lines (rubocop -A)
- naas_contract_spec.rb: add cluster.reload before check_cluster_health! in tests that create trees
- parametric_insurance_spec.rb: add cluster.reload before evaluate_daily_health! in tests that create trees

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix leaderboard delete: sync admin status on login + verify deletions

Root cause: Supabase RLS checks is_admin in the DB users table, but the
user's is_admin was false (created before admin check was added). The
delete call silently returned 0 rows with no error.

Fixes:
- loginUser() now syncs admin status for hardcoded admin emails on login
- deleteCompletion() and deleteUser() now verify rows were actually deleted
  and throw descriptive errors if RLS blocks the operation
- Added missing completions UPDATE RLS policy to supabase-setup.sql

After deploying, log out and log back in to sync admin status.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: settings LLM providers crash when key_env is None (local-mlx, ollama)

_builtin_providers() called os.environ.get(None) for providers with
key_env=None (local-mlx, ollama) → TypeError → 500 Internal Server Error
→ JSON parse failure in settings page.

Fix: use key_env or '' + check no_auth flag so local providers show as
'available' rather than 'no key'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v1.1.4 (#110)

* fix(scroll): normalize wheel delta for Firefox Linux snap scrolling

- convert wheel delta from line/page modes to pixel units before snap logic
- use normalized delta for direction and accumulator checks
- extract snap threshold into WHEEL_SNAP_THRESHOLD_PX constant

commit.message:
Add Blender export script for .fskel skeleton format

Python addon for Blender 3.6+/4.x that exports armature bone hierarchy,
rest transforms, all 20 Blender-compatible pose constraints, and inverse
bind matrices to the binary .fskel format.

Install via Edit > Preferences > Add-ons > Install from Disk.
Appears under File > Export > Talarium Skeleton (.fskel).

Handles: IK, Spline IK, Child Of, Copy Transforms/Rotation/Location/Scale,
Damped Track, Track To, Locked Track, Limit Rotation/Location/Scale,
Transformation, Action, Clamp To, Floor, Maintain Volume, Shrinkwrap, Pivot.

Verified: Python-written files round-trip through the C fskel_load() reader.

commit.message:
kernel: enable BPF_SYSCALL and CGROUP_BPF for Docker cgroup v2

Docker/runc uses bpf_prog_query(BPF_CGROUP_DEVICE) to manage device
access under cgroup v2. Without CONFIG_BPF_SYSCALL=y the syscall is not
compiled in and the call returns ENOSYS, causing every container start
to fail with:

  error setting cgroup config for procHooks process:
  bpf_prog_query(BPF_CGROUP_DEVICE) failed: function not implemented

The base kernel has CONFIG_BPF=y but CONFIG_BPF_SYSCALL was explicitly
disabled. Enable it along with CONFIG_CGROUP_BPF which gates the cgroup
device-filtering program type.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use received HTTP bytes (not decrypted) for EOF detection in windowed stream

window_got (decrypted bytes delivered to caller) understates the actual
server response when block_skip > 0 — i.e. when a TCP drop occurred at
a non-AES-block-boundary offset and the reconnect re-requests from an
earlier aligned position.  In that case window_got < _RANGE_WINDOW even
though the server sent a full window, causing the loop to return early
and truncate the stream.

Fix: compute received = window_got + block_skip (actual HTTP bytes from
the server) and use that for the EOF guard instead of window_got.

Add test_get_audio_stream_continues_after_non_block_boundary_drop:
- patches _RANGE_WINDOW to 32 bytes, 50-byte file
- drops at byte 17 (non-block boundary), reconnect delivers full 32-byte
  window from block_start=16, second window delivers remaining 2 bytes
- verifies all 50 bytes are received and exactly 3 HTTP requests are made

Ref: https://github.com/music-assistant/server/pull/3237#discussion_r2894243866

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: rsuvaidyam <rkrahul0001@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: abhinawsuvaidyam <abhinav.pratap@dhwaniris.com>
Co-authored-by: Bittu kumar <118291416+bittu9835@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Abhishek kumar <90375329+theabhirock74@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Abhishek Kumar <theabhirock74@br.lan>
Co-authored-by: Vishwash singh <singhvishwash776@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Aniket Singh <90967110+aniketsuvaidyam@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Samar Singh <samar.suvaidyam@gmail.com>

Fix TLS checkptr violation in test

The TestBlockInitialization test was creating a Go slice on the stack
and passing its address as InitData. This violates Go's checkptr rules
because:

1. Converting &slice[0] to uintptr and storing it breaks the
   'immediate use' pattern required for safe uintptr→unsafe.Pointer
2. The Go runtime's race detector correctly flags this as potentially
   unsafe pointer arithmetic

Fix: Use mmap.Map() to allocate the init data in kernel memory instead
of Go-managed memory. This matches real-world usage where InitData
points to a PT_LOAD segment from an ELF file.

The TLS implementation code in tls.go was correct - only the test
needed fixing. Added clarifying comment to Module.InitData field.

Fixes: go test -race ./internal/tls/ now passes
Tests: All tests pass with race detector enabled

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: build for ARM64 - AKS nodes are arm64 not amd64

ROOT CAUSE FOUND: AKS node pool uses ARM64 VMs, but all previous
builds targeted linux/amd64 architecture.

Changes:
- Dockerfile: Switch all base images to --platform=linux/arm64
- Dockerfile: Change dotnet publish from -r linux-x64 to -r linux-arm64
- Workflow: Add QEMU for cross-platform emulation on x86_64 runner
- Workflow: Add buildx with docker driver for ARM64 build support
- Workflow: Build with --platform linux/arm64

commit.message:
feat: implement graceful shutdown for the HTTP server (#1608)

- [x] Update `cmd/manael/main.go` to use `http.Server` with graceful
shutdown via `signal.NotifyContext()` and `srv.Shutdown()`
- [x] Add `ReadHeaderTimeout: 10 * time.Second` to the server for
security best practice
- [x] Build and verify the changes compile correctly (go vet exit 0)
- [x] Code review passed
- [x] CodeQL security scan: 0 alerts
- [x] Restore `website/go.mod` — accidentally deleted `require
github.com/google/docsy v0.10.0 // indirect` line has been restored

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Implement graceful shutdown for the HTTP
server</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> Currently, the application starts the server using
`http.ListenAndServe()` in `cmd/manael/main.go`. When the process is
terminated (e.g., via `SIGTERM` or `SIGINT`), it shuts down abruptly,
dropping any in-flight HTTP requests.
> 
> To improve reliability and follow modern Go best practices, we should
implement a graceful shutdown mechanism.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Update `cmd/manael/main.go` to use `http.Server.Shutdown()` in
combination with `signal.NotifyContext()` (introduced in Go 1.16). This
will allow the server to finish processing active requests before
exiting.
> 
> ### Example Implementation
> ```go
> package main
> 
> import (
> 	"context"
> 	"errors"
> 	"log"
> 	"net/http"
> 	"os/signal"
> 	"syscall"
> 	"time"
> )
> 
> func main() {
> 	// ... (setup handler, etc.)
> 
> 	srv := &http.Server{
> 		Addr:              conf.httpAddr,
> 		Handler:           handler,
> 		ReadHeaderTimeout: 10 * time.Second, // Security best practice
> 	}
> 
> ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt,
syscall.SIGTERM)
> 	defer stop()
> 
> 	go func() {
> 		log.Printf("Starting server on %s", conf.httpAddr)
> if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && !errors.Is(err,
http.ErrServerClosed) {
> 			log.Fatalf("Server error: %v", err)
> 		}
> 	}()
> 
> 	<-ctx.Done()
> 	stop()
> 	log.Println("Shutting down gracefully, press Ctrl+C again to force")
> 
> timeoutCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(),
5*time.Second)
> 	defer cancel()
> 
> 	if err := srv.Shutdown(timeoutCtx); err != nil {
> 		log.Fatalf("Server forced to shutdown: %v", err)
> 	}
> 
> 	log.Println("Server exiting")
> }
> ```</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

- Fixes manaelproxy/manael#1581

commit.message:
feat: add node_provisioning_profile support (#731)

* feat: add node_provisioning_profile support

Adds support for the node_provisioning_profile block to the azurerm_kubernetes_cluster resource.
This feature controls node provisioning behavior at the cluster level with two optional parameters:
- mode: Provisioning mode for node pools (Auto/Manual)
- default_node_pools: Whether default node pools auto-provision (Auto/None)

Includes three validation blocks to enforce:
- At least one field must be specified when block is configured
- mode must be either Auto or Manual
- default_node_pools must be either Auto or None

Changes:
- Added node_provisioning_profile variable to variables.tf with validations
- Added dynamic block to main.tf in azurerm_kubernetes_cluster resource
- Added outputs for node_provisioning_profile and node_provisioning_profile_enabled
- Updated startup example to demonstrate usage

Fixes #720

* chore: remove node_provisioning_profile outputs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix example and amend precondition

* fix: ensure node_provisioning_profile mode Auto is compatible with agent pool settings

When node_provisioning_profile.mode is set to Auto, Azure API requires
auto_scaling_enabled to be false on all agent pools. This commit:

- Fix startup example: set auto_scaling_enabled=false and agents_count=2,
  remove autoscaling-only params (agents_max_count, agents_min_count)
- Add precondition on azurerm_kubernetes_cluster to prevent
  node_provisioning_profile.mode=Auto with auto_scaling_enabled=true
- Add precondition on both extra node pool resources for the same check
- Add precondition to validate agents_count/node_count >= 1 when
  auto_scaling_enabled is false

---------

Co-authored-by: André Gomes <andre.gomes@promptlyhealth.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
